facebook.texto: I was sitting in the dental office parking lot on my lunch break with my Mounjaro® injector pen in my hand and a Post-it note on the dashboard that said "STOP." That was me three weeks ago. Forty-five minutes left of my lunch break, half a turkey sandwich uneaten on the passenger seat, and a decision to make. I was about to skip my next shot. Not officially. Not with a "discontinuation appointment" or a conversation with my prescriber. Just quietly. The way you quit something you're ashamed you

analise_criativa.avatar: Middle-aged woman, approximately 45-55 years old, warm appearance, light background — positioned as a relatable everyday woman who used Mounjaro and is now considering quitting it
analise_criativa.editing: Static Facebook ad library screenshot format. Profile photo of woman used as avatar. Ad creative partially visible showing what appears to be a kitchen or home setting. Minimal visual design — copy-heavy, personal narrative style.
analise_criativa.observation: The copy directly names Mounjaro by brand, which is unusual and bold. The narrative of 'quitting the shot quietly' taps into shame and secret behavior — very emotionally loaded. Headline at bottom reads 'She Quit Her Shots. Then Got Diabetic.' which is a strong fear-based hook. The page has multiple versions active suggesting active split testing.
analise_criativa.highlight: Extremely strong emotional hook targeting Mounjaro users experiencing doubt or side effects. The parking lot scene creates vivid, specific imagery. 'She Quit Her Shots. Then Got Diabetic.' headline is a high-curiosity fear driver that could generate strong CTR from GLP-1 users. High potential for natural mounjaro alternative bridge.

facebook.texto: Keep seeing ladies say the berberine black pepper combo is basically natural ozempic. is this legit? anyone tried it?

analise_criativa.avatar: Woman in her 40s sitting in a car, casual appearance, blonde hair, relatable everyday look — positioned as a curious consumer asking a genuine question about natural weight loss alternatives
analise_criativa.editing: Static image. Profile photo shows woman in car setting. The ad creative below is partially obscured — appears to show a minimal or illustrative graphic. Copy is written in casual first-person question format mimicking organic social post.
analise_criativa.observation: The copy is written as a genuine crowd-sourced question ('is this legit? anyone tried it?') which is a classic fake-organic native ad technique. No actual creative image is clearly visible beyond the profile photo. The berberine + black pepper framing directly piggybacks on Ozempic cultural awareness. Very short copy — unusually brief for this format.
analise_criativa.highlight: The 'natural ozempic' question format is a proven scroll-stopper for women aware of GLP-1 drugs but seeking cheaper or safer alternatives. The conversational tone mimics real Facebook group posts which can drive high engagement and curiosity clicks. Worth testing longer copy variants on this same avatar and angle.

facebook.texto: I lost 53 pounds on the shot and last week I almost threw the rest of the pen in the bin. I'm going to tell you why. Because this is the conversation nobody is having out loud, and I almost lost everything because of it. I'm Sarah. 44. Two kids. A husband who has loved me through every weight I've ever been, including the ones I haven't. For most of my adult life I was the woman who tries everything. WW. Optavia. Whole30.

analise_criativa.avatar: Woman approximately 40-50 years old, natural appearance, light neutral top, warm and approachable look — positioned as a relatable mother and wife sharing a vulnerable personal story about weight loss and GLP-1 shots
analise_criativa.editing: Static Facebook ad format. Profile photo of woman as main visual anchor. Ad creative partially visible showing what appears to be a dark moody lifestyle image — possibly an injection pen or personal item on a surface. Copy is long-form personal narrative.
analise_criativa.observation: Same page name 'Sarah Mitchell' as Ad 7 but different Library ID — this is a duplicate creative test with different image variants. The destination URL is tryblooomandbond.com suggesting a supplement brand. The hook 'almost threw the rest of the pen in the bin' is emotionally powerful. 45,000+ Happy Customers social proof shown in preview.
analise_criativa.highlight: Strong vulnerability-based hook targeting GLP-1 users mid-journey. The 53 pounds lost stat adds credibility. Running in GB market with EU transparency badge — compliant and mature campaign. The 'conversation nobody is having out loud' framing creates exclusivity and urgency to read more. High-value avatar to test.

facebook.texto: I lost 53 pounds on the shot and last week I almost threw the rest of the pen in the bin. I'm going to tell you why. Because this is the conversation nobody is having out loud, and I almost lost everything because of it. I'm Sarah. 44. Two kids. A husband who has loved me through every weight I've ever been, including the ones I haven't. For most of my adult life I was the woman who tries everything. WW. Optavia. Whole30.

analise_criativa.avatar: Same woman as Ad 6 — approximately 40-50 years old, natural appearance, light neutral top — same Sarah Mitchell persona used across split test variants targeting GLP-1 users in the UK market
analise_criativa.editing: Static Facebook ad format. Identical copy to Ad 6 but different Library ID and different creative image variant — this version appears to show a lighter, more neutral background image possibly featuring personal items or a pen on a surface. Same destination URL tryblooomandbond.com.
analise_criativa.observation: This is a direct creative split test of Ad 6 — same page, same copy, same avatar, different image. The fact that both are still active after 12 days suggests neither has been killed yet and the brand is still in testing phase. Both ads show identical social proof '45,000+ Happy Customers' in the link preview.
analise_criativa.highlight: Valuable to monitor which image variant wins between Ad 6 and Ad 7 — the copy hook is strong so the image difference will reveal what visual style resonates best with the GB natural mounjaro audience. Good benchmark for image creative testing methodology.

repeated_creatives.description: Identical avatar (Sarah Mitchell), identical opening copy ('I lost 53 pounds on the shot and last week I almost threw the rest of the pen in the bin...'), same keyword, same days active, same format — clear split-test variants of the same source video or near-identical creative

frequent_avatars.type: Relatable middle-aged woman (40s-50s), casual/natural appearance, speaking from personal experience with GLP-1 drugs or alternatives — positioned as an everyday consumer, not an expert

facebook_cross_analysis.dominant_mechanism: Natural GLP-1 mimicry — positioning plant-based or supplement ingredients (berberine, black pepper, etc.) as safer, accessible alternatives to prescription GLP-1 injections like Ozempic and Mounjaro
facebook_cross_analysis.emerging_angle: The GLP-1 quitter narrative — women who achieved significant weight loss on prescription shots but are now actively considering stopping, framed as an emotionally charged, underrepresented conversation that bridges GLP-1 users into natural supplement alternatives
facebook_cross_analysis.market_signal: Both US and UK markets are heavily testing UGC creatives that intercept existing GLP-1 drug users at the point of doubt or discontinuation, using relatable female personas to redirect them toward natural supplement alternatives positioned as equally effective without the side effects or cost
